'''NK-3''' was the designation given to two [[IG-100 MagnaGuard/Legends|IG-100 MagnaGuards]] that operated as the personal bodyguards of the [[N-K Necrosis]] droid on [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]].

==History==
The [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droid]] [[IG-72]] captured two MagnaGuards on behalf of the scientist [[Treun Lorn]], to be used as part of [[Nycolai Kinesworthy]]'s research with the remains of [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]]. The result was N-K Necrosis, a killing machine in the vein of Grievous himself. The NK-3s were used to train the Necrosis droid in the [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|forms]] Grievous had once instructed his guards.<ref name="Lord of War">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/86/indexp2.html|text=The Story of General Grievous: Lord of War|int=The Story of General Grievous: Lord of War}}</ref>

The NK-3s and Necrosis itself were ultimately defeated by a rag-tag band of [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] in the [[Myyydril Caverns/Legends|Myyydril Caverns]] on Kashyyyk. The droids' remains were stripped of all valuable possessions.<ref name="SWG:RotW" />

==Behind the scenes==
NK-3's appeared in the video game ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a massively multiplayer online role-playing game developed by Sony Online Entertainment and published by LucasArts, prior to its closure on [[December 15]], 2011.
